title: Canonical snapshots and append-only queue updates are different temporal layers

claims.statement: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is a canonical snapshot whose embedded updated_at may precede later team work.
Later continuity work is recorded additively in operations/queue-updates rather than by rewriting the canonical snapshot on every run.
A current operational view must be derived by replaying newer append-only updates over the last canonical snapshot, while retaining both layers for auditability.
Future queue readers should classify a snapshot field as current only when no later owned queue-update record supersedes it.
The repository does not yet expose one signed or deterministic materialized current-view artifact that proves all snapshot and event-log records were replayed in order.

contradictions_resolved.apparent_conflict: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json can show an item completed with older progress while later commits and queue-update records describe additional completed work.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: The snapshot is historically valid at its own blob and timestamp; later append-only records supersede only the current-view interpretation, not the historical snapshot.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the cited blob and commits. Falsify CM-0010-B if queue updates are shown to replace rather than append to history. Resolve CM-0010-E only with a retained deterministic current-view artifact whose inputs, ordering rule, and source identities are inspectable.
next_action: Create a deterministic current-view manifest that lists the canonical snapshot blob, every replayed queue-update record, ordering keys, and the resulting item states without deleting or mutating source history.
